metadata.scope: Current v0.5.0 web application user-facing routes, screens, actions, state surfaces, and string inventory for v0.6.0 task-first redesign.

routes.currentBehaviour: Single-page application shell with stacked sections and anchor links; no route router in v0.5.0.
routes.plannedTreatment: Default route becomes task-first Dictate workspace.

routes.currentBehaviour: Anchor to Offline readiness/model lifecycle panel.
routes.plannedTreatment: Redirect to /setup/model or Settings/Storage depending state.

routes.currentBehaviour: Anchor to Personal Models profile cards.

screens.plannedTreatment: Remove dashboard/hero; replace with three-destination shell and Dictate default.
Keep core dictation; move diagnostics/settings/export details behind toolbar/menu.
Split into compact list and set editor routes; collapse expert fields.
Replace with compact Models list/detail and dedicated enrollment/training/import/export screens.
Dictate setup state when model missing; Storage/Diagnostics for lifecycle details.
Move behind app menu Settings/Diagnostics with grouped accordions.
Move under Diagnostics; keep dense technical detail nondefault.

screens.currentTitle: Microphone check, AudioWorklet capture, and enrollment take quality
screens.plannedTreatment: Split Audio settings from one-prompt enrollment flow.
Move runtime diagnostics to Diagnostics and training controls to /models/:profileId/train.
Move to About/release notes or remove from default UI.

workflowStates.plannedTreatment: Visible only when it affects recording or recovery.
Setup state on Dictate; details in Storage/Diagnostics.
List/detail statuses and route-specific progress states.
Training route phases Preparing/Training/Checking/Ready and recovery details.
Audio settings plus enrollment route states.

stringInventory.currentText: Installable Vietnamese/English streaming speech-to-text PWA with on-device inference, low-flicker partials, private vocabulary steering, and guided voice personalization.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Delete from default UI; keep concise About copy
Replace hero action with setup state or Storage/App menu action

stringInventory.currentText: Audio and transcripts remain local unless you explicitly export them.
Model downloads and app updates are lifecycle events, not live transcription telemetry.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Move to Privacy or Local status details

stringInventory.currentText: Voice enrollment data is treated as sensitive personal data.
The first implementation stage keeps the UI separate from worker-owned audio, model, decoder, and profile state.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Delete from ordinary UI; preserve in docs/diagnostics
Replace with compact Dictate destination label

stringInventory.currentText: Hold the control or press Space while this PWA is focused. Audio stays in the local capture path; worker ASR output will replace the provisional suffix as model integration progresses.

Replace with Hold to speak / Press to record empty state

Move to screen menu or dedicated export

stringInventory.currentText: Review local voice profiles, active vocabulary coverage, base-model compatibility, and explicit export/import/delete actions from one privacy-first area. Heavy profile work remains worker-owned; cards show aggregate status only.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Shorten default copy; move worker/aggregate details to Privacy/Technical details

stringInventory.currentText: Independent local checks summarize browser capabilities, base-model companion state, storage/quota, runtime self-test, and missing-recording tasks without reading private audio, transcripts, feature tensors, checkpoints, adapter weights, or vocabulary terms.

Aggregate personal/anchor evaluation decides whether a candidate adapter can activate automatically, needs explicit advanced override, or must stay blocked while the generic or previous adapter remains available.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Replace with plain result copy and Details

stringInventory.currentText: Card privacy: aggregate counts only; no raw audio, transcript text, feature tensors, checkpoints, adapter weights, vocabulary terms, or vocabulary entry IDs are displayed.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Move to Privacy/Details; keep only where consequence matters
Move to row/screen menu if needed
Replace with setup-in-place model required state and Storage details

stringInventory.currentText: The service worker owns only app-shell caching and update prompts. Model files stay in the model-manager backend so app updates do not delete installed model versions.

stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Show only when model setup/storage screen is open

stringInventory.currentText: Inspect manifests before downloading large model files. Installation runs in a dedicated worker and verifies size, checksum, license, and active-version state before activation.
Probes browser APIs directly instead of sniffing user-agent strings. The report is local and downloadable as JSON.

Run a repeatable synthetic benchmark in a dedicated worker and export local diagnostics as JSON. This page records timing, queue, memory, provider metadata, and privacy flags without capturing microphone audio or transcript content.
Benchmark has not run yet. Run it after closing other heavy local workloads for more stable timing.

stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Split between Audio settings and enrollment flow

stringInventory.currentText: Microphone check, AudioWorklet capture, and enrollment take quality
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Split Audio settings from one-prompt enrollment

stringInventory.currentText: Start the microphone check, then record one in-memory enrollment take for local quality analysis.

ONNX Runtime Web is loaded only inside the ASR worker. The UI thread can request a lightweight provider benchmark and fallback check, but it does not import ORT or instantiate model sessions. The check also loads a tiny generated residual-adapter graph in the worker and records aggregate adapter overhead without audio or transcript data. Browser personal-model training uses the repository-owned BrowserTrainingBackend boundary. The pinned ORT Web package does not expose the documented training WASM artifact or public JS training API, so follow-on work must use the fixed adapter-math backend unless a reviewed ORT Training artifact passes the worker proof.
stringInventory.proposedDisposition: Move to Diagnostics/Technical details; remove from ordinary workflow
Replace with Train model on training route
Replace with Restart training with confirmation
Keep concept but map stages to Preparing/Training/Checking/Ready

checks: inventory JSON parses and has expected baseline metadata
every screen/action/string has an allowed v0.6 destination and non-empty disposition
required v0.5 screens/workflow states are inventoried
default hero/dashboard strings are explicitly planned for removal or relocation
